Title: Draft Management — Save Push Message as Draft
Preconditions: Push Messages module installed and accessible at {{BACK_URL}}; admin user authenticated
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/push-messages
[BLADE] wait for Push Messages list blade to open
[ACT] click 'Add' or 'Create' button
[BLADE] wait for new message form blade to open
[ACT] fill message title field
[ACT] fill message body/content field
[SAVE] click 'Save as Draft'
[WAIT] success confirmation visible
Assertions:
[TOAST] success notification appears after save
[GRID] draft message appears in message list with status 'Draft'
[FORM] unsaved-changes dialog appears if navigating away without saving
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/platform/pushnotifications returns the saved draft with status=Draft
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on save request
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during save
Failure_Signals: Spinner visible >10s after save, 4xx/5xx on save API call, console TypeError on save action, blade does not close after save
Cleanup: Delete draft message after test via Admin grid delete action

Title: Draft Management — Validation on Empty Fields (Negative)
Preconditions: Push Messages module installed; admin user authenticated; new message form is open
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/push-messages
[BLADE] wait for Push Messages list blade to open
[ACT] click 'Add' or 'Create' button
[BLADE] wait for new message form blade to open
[ACT] leave all required fields empty
[SAVE] click 'Save' or 'Save as Draft'
[WAIT] validation response visible
Assertions:
[FORM] validation error messages appear on required empty fields
[FORM] save operation is prevented — blade remains open
[DOM] error indicator on each empty required field
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no POST request fired to create endpoint (or POST returns 400)
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during validation trigger
Failure_Signals: Save proceeds with empty fields, no validation error shown, 500 error on save attempt
Cleanup: none

Title: Draft Management — Save Draft with Schedule Date

Title: Recipient Tracking — New Recipients Receive Message When Enabled
Preconditions: A published push message exists with 'Track New Recipients' enabled; admin user authenticated; at least one new user exists who joined after publication
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/push-messages
[BLADE] wait for list blade to open
[GRID] locate published message with Track New Recipients = ON
[ACT] click message to open detail
[BLADE] wait for detail blade
[ACT] verify 'Track New Recipients' toggle is ON
[ASSERT] confirm at least one new user account was created after message publication
[ACT] check that new user's push message inbox contains this message
Assertions:
[FORM] 'Track New Recipients' toggle shows ON state
[DOM] new user's notification inbox contains the tracked message
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/platform/pushnotifications for new user returns the tracked message
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
Failure_Signals: New user's inbox does not contain message despite tracking enabled, toggle state not persisted
Cleanup: none

Title: Recipient Tracking — New Recipients Do NOT Receive Message When Disabled
Preconditions: A published push message exists with 'Track New Recipients' disabled; admin user authenticated; a new user account can be created
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/push-messages
[BLADE] wait for list blade to open
[GRID] locate published message
[ACT] click message to open detail
[BLADE] wait for detail blade
[ACT] confirm 'Track New Recipients' toggle is OFF
[ASSERT] set toggle to OFF and save if not already
[ACT] create or identify a new user who joined after the message was published
[ACT] check new user's push notification inbox
Assertions:
[FORM] 'Track New Recipients' toggle shows OFF state
[DOM] new user's notification inbox does NOT contain this message
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/platform/pushnotifications for new user returns empty or does not contain this message
[NETWORK] no delivery request for new user after toggle=OFF
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
Failure_Signals: New user receives message despite tracking disabled, 4xx/5xx on toggle save
Cleanup: Delete test user account if created during test

Title: Storefront Notifications — Targeting: User Receives Only Own Messages
Preconditions: Two user accounts exist (User A and User B); a push message was published targeting only User A's role or user ID; both users are accessible for testing
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/notifications
[ACT] sign in as {{USER_EMAIL}} (User A — targeted user)
[WAIT] authenticated session established
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/notifications
[WAIT] page loads
[ASSERT] note the targeted push message is visible in User A's notifications list
[ACT] sign out
[ACT] sign in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}} (User B — non-targeted user)
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/notifications
[WAIT] page loads
Assertions:
[STATE] User A can see the push message targeted to their role/ID in the notifications list
[STATE] User B does NOT see the push message targeted only to User A's role/ID
[DOM] User B's notification list excludes the targeted message
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/pushnotifications for User B's token does not return the targeted message
[CONSOLE] no JS errors for either user session
Failure_Signals: User B sees User A's targeted messages (security/isolation failure), targeted message absent for User A, 4xx on either user's notification fetch
Cleanup: Sign out as User B after test; no data mutation

Title: Storefront — Clear All Notifications (Bell Dropdown) and Mark Single as Unread

[WAIT] notifications page loads

Assertions:
"[DOM] 'Clear all' action is present in bell dropdown

Title: Storefront — Real-Time Delivery via SignalR Without Page Reload
Preconditions: "User {{USER_EMAIL}} logged in on storefront; pushNotificationHub SignalR connection is active; admin can create and send a push message targeting {{USER_EMAIL}} in real time"
Test_Data: "front_url={{FRONT_URL}}, back_url={{BACK_URL}}, store_id={{STORE_ID}}, admin_email={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, admin_password={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, user_email={{USER_EMAIL}}, user_password={{USER_PASSWORD}}"
Steps:
"--- STOREFRONT ---
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] sign in as {{USER_EMAIL}} with {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[WAIT] authenticated session — bell badge visible
[ASSERT] record initial bell badge unread count
--- ADMIN (setup — send targeted message via API, admin-authenticated) ---
[API] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/push-message  body: {"topic":"AGENT-TEST-realtime","shortMessage":"AGENT-TEST-realtime-body","status":"Sent","memberIds":["{{TEST_USER_ID}}"]} -> 200 (message created+sent)
--- STOREFRONT (observe — no page reload) ---
[WAIT] up to 10 seconds for bell badge to update without page reload"
Assertions:
"[DOM] bell badge count increments by 1 within 10s of admin send — without page reload
[STATE] clicking bell dropdown reveals the new AGENT-TEST-realtime-body message in the list
[DOM] new message appears at top of dropdown (newest first)"
Cross_Layer_Checks:
"[NETWORK] SignalR pushNotificationHub WebSocket connection is active (ws:// or wss:// frame visible in network tab)
[API] xAPI pushMessages(unreadOnly: true) confirms new message in inbox after real-time delivery
[CONSOLE] no JS errors; no SignalR connection-error events"
Failure_Signals: "Bell badge does not increment after send (SignalR not connected), badge only updates on page reload, new message absent from dropdown after badge update, AGENT-TEST-realtime-body not in xAPI pushMessages response, console SignalR error"
Cleanup: "REST DELETE {{BACK_URL}}/api/push-message?ids={{RT_MSG_ID}} (will return 500 if Sent — acceptable; log and proceed); sign out"

Title: Storefront — Notification Link Navigates to Linked Entity (Order / Quote / Shipment)
Preconditions: "User authenticated; at least one push message with a link to an order, quote, or shipment exists in the user's inbox (not only category links). NOTE: push-message model has no dedicated link field — a working link requires an HTML <a> anchor embedded directly in shortMessage; self-seed via admin POST /api/push-message if no such message exists."
Steps:
"[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/notifications
[ACT] sign in as {{USER_EMAIL}} with {{USER_PASSWORD}} if not already
[WAIT] notifications page loads
[ACT] scan notification items for any that contain an embedded URL or action button referencing /account/orders/, /account/quotes/, or /account/shipments/
[ASSERT] at least one such notification is visible
[ACT] click the notification link or 'View [entity]' button
[WAIT] navigation completes — target page loads"
Assertions:
"[NAV] URL navigates to a path matching /account/orders/{id} OR /account/quotes/{id} OR /account/shipments/{id} — NOT a category link
[DOM] target entity page loads with entity details visible (order number, quote ID, or shipment details)
[DOM] entity referenced in notification matches the entity displayed on the target page"
Cross_Layer_Checks:
"[NETWORK] no 4xx on navigation to linked entity (linked entity must exist)
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during link click or navigation"
Failure_Signals: "Link navigates to /catalog or other non-entity page (wrong entity scope), 404 on entity page, entity page blank, entity ID in URL does not match notification content"
